diagrams.title: Cohen-Sutherland Line Clipping Algorithm
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the Cohen-Sutherland algorithm for line clipping against a rectangular clipping window. Shows the 9 regions with their outcodes (4-bit binary codes), a sample line segment passing through the window, and the clipping process at intersection points.
elements: Clipping window rectangle centered in viewport
9 grid regions labeled with outcodes (0000 to 1111)
Original line segment (both endpoints outside)
Intersection points where line crosses window edges
Clipped line segment inside the window
Color legend: blue for original line, green for clipped result, red for intersection points
diagrams.purpose: Explains how Cohen-Sutherland uses outcodes to quickly determine whether lines are completely inside, completely outside, or require clipping at window boundaries

diagrams.title: Sutherland-Hodgman Polygon Clipping
diagrams.description: Shows the Sutherland-Hodgman polygon clipping algorithm in 4 sequential stages: clipping against left, right, top, and bottom edges of a rectangular window. Displays how each edge processes the polygon vertices and produces new vertex sequences.
elements: Rectangular clipping window with labeled edges
Original polygon (complex shape crossing all 4 edges)
Stage 1: After left edge clipping
Stage 2: After right edge clipping
Stage 3: After top edge clipping
Stage 4: Final clipped polygon after bottom edge
Vertex input/output arrows showing the process
Color gradient showing progression through stages
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how Sutherland-Hodgman algorithm successively clips a polygon against each edge of the window, producing a new vertex list at each stage

diagrams.title: Liang-Barsky Line Clipping Parametric View
diagrams.description: Illustrates the Liang-Barsky parametric line clipping algorithm showing the line equation P(t) = P1 + t(P2-P1), the parameter t values for entry (t1) and exit (t2) points, and how inequalities define the clipping region boundaries.
elements: Coordinate axes with clipping window bounds (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
Line segment P1-P2 with parameter labels
Parameter t scale (0 to 1) showing t1 and t2 positions
Entry/exit point indicators
Mathematical inequalities panel showing p and q values
Clipped result visualization
Color coding: yellow for input parameters, cyan for t1/t2, magenta for clipped segment
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the parametric approach of Liang-Barsky algorithm where line clipping is reduced to solving linear inequalities to find entry and exit parameters
